---
title: "Level 3: AI in Your Terminal"
---

At Level 3, the AI runs in your terminal. You describe a goal; it reads your files, writes code, runs scripts, and iterates on failures until the task is done. You review the result. What makes this more capable than Level 2 is not what the agent can see — it is that it can execute. At Level 2, you run the tests and bring errors back to the AI. At Level 3, the agent runs that loop itself.

> **Most terminal AI tools require a paid subscription.** Free tiers typically provide access to web-based chat only — not the terminal agent. Check the pricing for whichever tool you use before getting started.

Level 3 is the right tool when the task requires execution — running tests, iterating across files, working through a multi-step process. For inline suggestions while you're actively writing code, the IDE is still the natural fit. The two methods complement each other; most people use both.
